subject: Chemistry
title: Dalton's Atomic Theory
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Dalton's Atomic Theory.

hook.text: In 1803, John Dalton proposed that each element is made of unique atoms with a specific weight. Modern chemistry began.

buildup.text: Dalton noticed that elements combine in fixed ratios by weight—hydrogen and oxygen always combine as 1:8.

discovery.text: He concluded: atoms of each element are identical, atoms of different elements differ in weight and properties.

twist.text: Dalton was colorblind—literally. He studied his own condition and 'daltonism' became a word for colorblindness.

climax.text: His atomic weights were often wrong, but the concept was revolutionary. Chemistry became quantitative, not qualitative.

punchline.text: Fixed ratios mean fixed atoms. Dalton turned chemistry into a counting science.

quiz.question: What key observation led Dalton to propose atomic theory?
options: Elements combine in fixed ratios by weight
All gases expand at the same rate when heated
Elements have unique colors when burned
